segments.text: Then went Samson to Gaza, and saw there an harlot, and went in unto her.

footnotes: harlot: Heb. a woman an harlot

segments.text: And it was told the Gazites, saying, Samson is come hither.
In the morning, when it is day, we shall kill him.
segments.speaker: Gazites

segments.text: And Samson lay till midnight, and arose at midnight, and took the doors of the gate of the city, and the two posts, and went away with them, bar and all, and put them upon his shoulders, and carried them up to the top of an hill that is before Hebron.
And it came to pass afterward, that he loved a woman in the valley of Sorek, whose name was Delilah.

Entice him, and see wherein his great strength lieth, and by what means we may prevail against him, that we may bind him to afflict him: and we will give thee every one of us eleven hundred pieces of silver.
segments.speaker: Lords of the Philistines

segments.text: Tell me, I pray thee, wherein thy great strength lieth, and wherewith thou mightest be bound to afflict thee.
segments.speaker: Delilah

segments.text: If they bind me with seven green withs that were never dried, then shall I be weak, and be as another man.
segments.speaker: Samson

footnotes: green…: or, new cords: Heb. moist

segments.text: O Lord GOD, remember me, I pray thee, and strengthen me, I pray thee, only this once, O God, that I may be at once avenged of the Philistines for my two eyes.
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: Let me die with the Philistines.
segments.speaker: Samson

segments.text: And he bowed himself with all his might; and the house fell upon the lords, and upon all the people that were therein. So the dead which he slew at his death were more than they which he slew in his life.

Then his brethren and all the house of his father came down, and took him, and brought him up, and buried him between Zorah and Eshtaol in the buryingplace of Manoah his father. And he judged Israel twenty years.
